title: Supporting the Second Amendment's guarantee that the right of the people to keep and bear arms shall not be infringed, and commending President Trump and his administration as they work to protect Second Amendment freedoms by reviewing and eliminating any of the Biden administration's infringements on American's constitutional freedoms.
plain_summary: This bill is about supporting Americans' right to own guns, which is protected by the Second Amendment to the Constitution. The bill criticizes President Biden's administration for making rules that make it harder for people to own and carry guns. It praises President Trump for working to get rid of these restrictions and protect people's gun rights. The bill says these gun rights are an important freedom that should not be taken away from Americans.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provi

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution supports the Second Amendment's promise that people have the right to own and carry guns, and this right cannot be taken away. The resolution also praises President Trump and his team for working to protect Second Amendment rights by looking at and getting rid of any restrictions the Biden administration put on Americans' constitutional freedoms.

sections.title: Sponsors and Committees
sections.text: On April seventeenth, two thousand twenty-five, Congressman Williams of Texas and sixteen other representatives introduced this resolution. The resolution was sent to the Committee on the Judiciary and the Committee on Foreign Affairs for review.

sections.title: Background Statements
sections.text: The resolution explains several key points. The Second Amendment protects people's right to own and carry guns and says this right cannot be taken away. James Madison, who wrote the Second Amendment, said Americans have the right to own guns unlike people in other countries whose governments don't trust them with weapons. The resolution states that the Biden administration wanted to limit and make it harder for regular law-abiding people to use their right to own guns. It claims Biden encouraged lawmakers at all levels to pass gun control laws that go against the Second Amendment, even though the Second Amendment helps protect against crime, unfair government control, and oppression. The resolution says the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (a government agency that regulates guns) created more rules in recent years to limit Americans' gun rights. It also says the Bureau of Industry and Security limited Second Amendment rights by creating policies that restrict selling guns to other countries without giving good reasons why this ban is needed. The resolution states that President Trump has strongly defended people's right to own and buy guns by removing Biden's gun control measures. It says Trump is protecting law-abiding gun owners from harmful policies by these government agencies. The resolution concludes that Trump continues to support law-abiding gun owners and gun makers to make sure the Second Amendment stays protected.

sections.title: House of Representatives Resolution
sections.text: The House of Representatives makes six official statements. First, it reaffirms support for the Second Amendment and the constitutional promise that people's right to own and carry guns cannot be taken away. Second, it disapproves of the Biden administration's gun control plans and unfriendly attitude toward the legal gun industry. Third, it disapproves of rule and policy changes made by the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives under the Biden administration that restricted citizens' Second Amendment rights. Fourth, it disapproves of the Bureau of Industry and Security policies that limit legal gun exports and unfair attacks on civilian gun ownership. Fifth, it supports President Trump's actions to cancel harmful policies the Biden administration put on law-abiding gun owners. Sixth, it praises President Trump's commitment to upholding the Constitution and defending the constitutional right to own guns.
